卓越（紫色）：之前是-20瞄准速度，+50射程及+50瞄准精准度，现为-20瞄准速度，+60射程及+60移动时的精准度 瞄具1.4x 强化（绿色）：之前是+20瞄准精准度及+20移动时的精准度，现为+20瞄准速度及+20瞄准精准度 豪华（蓝色）：之前是+30瞄准精准度及+50移动时的精准度，现为+40瞄准速度及+40瞄准精准度 卓越（紫色）：之前是+50瞄准精准度及+80移动时的精准度，现为+60瞄准速度及+60瞄准精准度 清视镜片 强化（绿色）：之前是+30瞄准速度及+15瞄准精准度，现为+10瞄准精准度，+10射程及+10移动时的精准度 豪华（蓝色）：之前是+50瞄准速度及+25瞄准精准度，现为+30瞄准精准度，+20射程及+20移动时的精准度 卓越（紫色）：之前是+70瞄准速度及+35瞄准精准度，现为+70瞄准精准度，+40射程及+30移动时的精准度 测距仪瞄具1.3x（测距仪） 强化（绿色）：之前是+20瞄准精准度及+20蹲伏精准度，现为+10射程，+10瞄准精准度及+10蹲伏精准度 豪华（蓝色）：之前是+30瞄准精准度及+30蹲伏精准度，现为+20射程，+20瞄准精准度及+20蹲伏精准度 卓越（紫色）：之前是+50瞄准精准度及+50蹲伏精准度，现为+40射程，+40瞄准精准度及+40蹲伏精准度 装备 手雷准备动作延长0.2秒。 开发者提示： 准备动作指的是拔出安全销准备投掷的过程，投掷动作（松开按钮后）不变。我们相信这处更改可以保留投掷手雷的敏捷度和乐趣体验，但同时也能在相当程度上限制玩家们快速连续投掷手雷的数量。延长时间也能让我们打磨这个准备动作的动画，使其更具表现力。 全新 ——冰霜地雷 和阔刀地雷类似，冰霜地雷是一种可放置装备，当敌人接近时便会引爆。它不会像阔刀地雷那样对锥形空间内的目标造成大量伤害，而是在球形空间内爆炸，造成中等伤害，但同时也会对所有爆炸波及的目标造成结霜减益。 全新 ——矢量手雷 这种手雷的主要功能并非造成伤害，而是收集信息。当矢量手雷爆炸时，它会释放出一片探测脉冲，并在场景中存在一小段时间，高亮显示大范围区域内的空间，以及处于其探测半径内的所有敌人。这种手雷是在夜间沼泽和其他视野受限环境中行动的必备伴侣。 全新 ——信号弹 信号弹包含红色、绿色、蓝色三种，每种都有不同的镜头光晕形状。 破片手雷 最大伤害降低15%（135→115）。 热量手雷 伤害降低50%（40→20）。 信号干扰器 当玩家执行动作、使用技能、使用武器射击或受到伤害时，视觉和探测干扰会短暂关停。 不再干扰预警系统（尊享侦察核心）的探测。 不再干扰必要情报（风行者M1T尊享瞄准镜模组）的探测。 开发者提示： 在第1赛季中，信号干扰器似乎成了每场战斗中的标配，因为不论你是调整站位准备伏击，还是直接冲入战斗之中，这件装备都能够确保提供直截了当的优势。这次更改能够让信号干扰器为战术性移动和潜行提供更多回报，同时在交火中的辅助作用又不那么强大。 战斗人员与环境危害 非看守者UESC幽灵的武器伤害降低了约20%。 缩小了有毒植物的激活半径，并更新了地面视觉效果，使其更容易被识别。 移除了编译者死亡时的伤害，但疾行者仍然会被击退。 修复了焦灼烧看守者的猛击可以隔墙发动的问题。 UESC侦察兵的热能冲击减少了镜头位移。 用户界面和体验 玩家现在可以在保险库界面点击可装备的配装栏位，仅显示保险库中可适配该栏位的物品。 简化了物品图标的视觉效果 玩家现在可以使用键盘/控制器快捷键在阵营升级界面购买升级。 默认按键绑定： 控制器：PS5：正方键 | Xbox：X 鼠标和键盘：空格 现在，疾行报告会显示通过疾行中的事件和目标获得的奖励。 优化了阵营菜单的UI性能。 增加了数据比较提示。 更新了死亡箱锁定图标，使其视觉风格更加一致。 修复了购买后奢享币余额可能暂时显示错误剩余余额的问题。 修复了从百科条目追踪的物品无法在其他地方取消追踪的问题。 修复了阵营升级期间奖励无法预览的问题。 修复了未出售物品上显示超限出售图标的错误。 修复了升级庆祝动画中可能出现的视觉异常。 修复了交互路径点有时会出现在空容器上的问题。 自定义 所有疾行者、武器和个人资料物品的默认装饰现在都可以在自定义中查看。玩家可以通过装备默认选项（而​​不是“卸下”当前装饰）将装饰重置为默认外观。 默认装饰不再像“新”物品一样显示自定义通知。 物品预览现可更自由地旋转，以便从更多角度查看。 将自定义菜单从保险库菜单中分离出来，并添加到主菜单。 在自定义中新增了“配件”部分，疾行者可以在此处查看挂件和贴纸收藏。 菜单导航 新增了对大厅内匹配的导航支持。 疾行准备界面（区域选择和准备就绪）进行了优化和改进，提升了用户体验。 地图变体和游戏模式之间的区分更加清晰。 选择团队规模和补位选项的方式更加清晰。 进一步优化了准备体验的视觉效果。 移除了配装菜单，以简化和加快疾行准备流程。 新增并重新排列了主菜单按钮，以便更快地访问主要疾行准备系统。 更新了底部固定栏按钮的显示方式，使其更加简洁明了。 军械库 提升了军械库的界面性能。 军械库中可免费领取的武器现在会显示模组稀有度指示器。 修复了领取每日免费物品后，军械库物品不显示购买要求的问题。 修复了在军械库购买需要材料花费的物品交换后，保险库中会残留多个未合并的部分材料叠加的问题。 修复了军械库易物中不显示材料花费的问题。 带有出售价值的赞助包现在会显示出售操作，而不是错误的0信用点拆解操作。 社交 改进了在大厅中使用按键通话的稳定性。 新增了在玩家个人资料界面静音玩家的功能。 更新了队员提示，当鼠标悬停在自己身上时会显示“启用/禁用文本聊天”。 修复了玩家在准备界面加入团队时，物品栏中的物品不会显示的问题。 队长现可在区域选择后通过下拉菜单控制团队人数。 好友列表的“最近玩家”选项卡现会将最近一次疾行的玩家显示在顶部，并修复了好友在最近玩家列表中顺序错乱的问题。 当你有待处理的邀请时，好友列表会在你首次打开时自动跳转到“所有邀请”选项卡。 带有未领取附件的邮箱消息现会在删除前显示确认对话框。 游戏内好友列表现应能正确显示《失落星船：马拉松》在线玩家，并且不会包含来自其他Bungie游戏的好友。 修复了玩家在准备界面无法看到其他队员武器自定义的问题。 修复了在超宽屏显示器上，“举报玩家”对话框内容超出标题安全区域的问题。 合约 为最新季度内容添加了合约标签。 修复了多个合约问题，包括合约目标地点指引错误、提示文本不清晰或不准确，以及路径点行为异常等。 修复了合约语音播放异常的问题，包括错误播放给多个团队，或在同一场疾行中多个团队完成合约时仅播放给其中一个团队。 修复了摧毁炮台或无人机不计入多个合约的问题。 修复了疾行者在领取之前合约已过期，之后无法在后续周期重新获得疾行者枪手低温档案室合约的问题。 进度 优先合约不再设置阵营等级要求。调整了标准合约池随等级升降而轮换的门槛。 根据当前激活的阵营合约，击杀UESC将为对应阵营带来少量声望。 下调了多个合约的难度，以减少完成它们所需的疾行次数。 开发者提示： 我们的目标是消除那些不合理的限制，减少大量跑图和“在一次疾行中”等要求，重新调整优先合约，让它们不会成为你进度上的绊脚石。 赛博艾克米 修复了解构III合约中豪华（蓝色）和卓越（紫色）回收资源与标准（灰色）回收资源进度相同的问题。同时，在提示中明确了哪些类型的回收资源会推进合约进度。 纽卡洛里 修复了范式偏转合约会干扰演化压力合约的问题。 修复了队友使用伏特电池武器击杀敌人时，无法推进电能突击I首个目标进度的问题。 特莱克斯 修复了投资回报率合约中，如果一个队员在骇入容器后死亡，另一个队员将无法与之互动的问题。 修复了无规冲突3/5中，同一团队中的疾行者可以多次扫描同一异常点，导致只生成一个样本并阻止合约完成的问题。 米达 修复了在米达信任/谎言2/6合约期间，疾行者可能被困在无人机区障碍物后的问题。 关口基因 修复了在寄生关系1/5中，在前哨完成DCON步骤会撤销进度的问题。 修复了救援的主要技能无法获得标准合约III进度的问题。 修复了侦察的追踪无人机在演化I时无法获得技能击杀进度的问题。 修复了协同进化4/5中，共享合成拟生体的目标进度可能错误奖励给完成合成/注射步骤的疾行者，而非奖励给正确疾行者的问题。 修复了疾行者可通过两次使用生物研究所合成仪/提取机来阻止寄生关系3/5（以及协同进化2/5）合约的进度，导致所需样本无法弹出的问题。 区域 修复了各种漂浮/错位的几何体以及激光等危险物的问题。 修复了某些物体表面可能出现的闪烁问题。同时修复了一些命中判定检测和/或物理效果不正确的问题。 修复了多处损坏的梯子和堵塞的通风口。 修复了多个区域隐形墙阻碍疾行者移动的问题。 修复了死亡箱可能生成在地图边界外或无无法触及区域的问题。 全新 ——恐怖沼泽（夜间） 新区域引入了全新机制、战斗人员以及探索地点。 外围区域 在拦截活动中新增了在疾行中保证获得背包的途径。 修复了在拖运站地图中，疾行者躲藏在错误物理效果区域而不被发现的漏洞。 恐怖沼泽 现在可以在藻池找到皮肤化学和神经药剂回收资源。 修复了封锁若先前在综合设施生成，则不会在藻池中生成的问题，反之亦然。 修复了生物研究所二楼的提取器在放入错误物品或空置后会永久无法交互的问题。 修复了最终撤离不会在沼泽生成的问题。 修复了爬入某些区域会导致疾行者受到伤害并死亡的问题。 前哨 修复了标记护航队会在2D地图上创建双重路径点的问题。 修复了热量级联会穿透天花板或物体的问题。 低温档案室 调整了低温档案室合约，使其更贴合低温机制，而非要求完成撤离和保险库任务。 开发者提示： 在第2赛季中，我们重做了此类合约，设定了更易实现的目标。你可以在多次疾行中累积推进目标，也帮助低温新手们了解飞船上的机制。不过，第七个合约仍然需要你击败编译者。 修复了4号保险库的门错误显示为可交互的问题。 修复了部分玩家即使满足条件也无法进入低温档案室的问题。 修复了部分掉落的战利品会穿过地板的问题。 修复了玩家可能卡在游戏世界中或掉落/看到地图边界外多个区域的问题。 修复了在低温档案室中，泵站凭证生成位置距离其他物体过近而导致无法获得的问题。 稳定性 实装了CPU性能优化 这只是我们计划推出的众多优化中的第一步，欢迎继续向我们提交反馈。 加入了支持PC平台的帧生成技术。 帧生成通过基于现有数据生成额外帧，从而提高帧率。 包含DLSS（NVIDIA）、FSR（AMD）和 XeSS（Intel）。 建议仅在当前渲染帧率已达到每秒60帧或更高时，才启用帧生成。在较低帧率下使用帧生成可能会导致输入延迟与影响画面质量。 NVIDIA DLSS需要GeForce RTX 40系列或更高显卡，并需在Windows设置中启用硬件加速GPU计划。 本地化 阵营介绍视频中的文本现已添加本地化字幕。

enUs.title: Open Play Week: June 2 - June 9
enUs.subtitle: Check out the full game this week, including all of Season 2 and the new experimental mode
enUs.author: Marathon Dev Team
enUs.bodyHtml:
Open Play Week Begins June 2 

At the start of Season 2, we’re opening the gates of Marathon so everyone can experience the new content and the many improvements we have made throughout Season 1. This will be a great time to invite your friends to crew up or check the game out for yourself, as everyone will be starting out on a fresh page and learning what’s new together.

During the Open Play Week* from June 2 to June 9, you can experience everything the new season has to offer, including the new Night Marsh zone, a new Runner shell, new gear, and more. Anyone who chooses to purchase will keep all items and progression after the Open Play Week ends. As a reminder, for those who already own Marathon, Season 2 gameplay content will be available for no additional cost.

Dive into what’s new in Season 2:

  • Season 2 Developer Insights Video
  • Welcome to Season 2 Article
  • Season 2 Sandbox Changes Article
  • Season 2 Cinematic Trailer

New Experimental Mode: Sponsored Survival

The start of Season 2 also brings a new experimental mode to Marathon from June 2 to June 9. As mentioned in our recent post, we want to explore more survival experiences for different moods, like if you want to go full sweat or would rather lean back and chill. This new experimental mode lands somewhere in between, and is a more PVP-lite and low risk way to play Marathon than typical runs.

In Sponsored Survival, a single crew will infil onto the new Night Marsh zone. They’ll start as the only players on the map and can explore, loot, and blast their flashlights without fear of repercussion... from PVP enemies anyways. The UESC will still be there in full force, as will some new enemies that lurk in the shadows.

After a certain period of time, backfilled Rooks can enter the map and begin their own scavenging excursions. Perhaps they’ll remain lone wolves, or maybe they’ll team up with each other or the sole crew on the map—everyone's goal is survival, and how you get there is up to you.

In this mode, your one and only ticket off the map is to survive to the final exfil. Each match has an 18-minute run timer, and once it hits zero, the final exfil will spawn. Everyone will have a limited amount of time to book it over and prepare to make their exit. Once there, the decision is yours: Will you negotiate a temporary truce and exfil as one, or turn your weapons on each other in the dead of night?

This asymmetrical mode changes the dynamics of a run, taking the heat off of the lone crew for the first few minutes of the match, while also keeping some of the fun, social unpredictability that Rooks bring to runs. Crews are required to bring a Sponsored Kit loadout to play, which keeps the stakes low for the crew, while also giving Rooks the peace of mind of knowing there’s no fully kitted-out teams on the map.

When you play as the crew, you can use queue fill to find teammates, or disable it to play as a duo or solo. While this mode is running, other Rook queues will be disabled and all Rooks will filter into Sponsored Survival.

Like our previous experimental modes—Duos Queue and Sponsored Queue—Sponsored Survival gives us a chance to test the waters with a different way to play Marathon. Each of these experiments has the potential to inform future experiments, core ways to play, or permanent additions to the game. We’ll also be testing a PVE mode in the latter half of Season 2, with more experiments to come later, such as perhaps a more purely PVP-focused mode.

Shoutout to everyone who’s participated in the experimental queues so far, and please keep your thoughts and feedback coming.

Announcing Season 2 Twitch Drops

Season 2 also brings a new set of Twitch Drops, available from June 2 at 10am PT to June 9 at 10am PT. There are five new rewards available for tuning in to anyone streaming in the Marathon category, and one reward available for gifting a sub.

Twitch Drop Rewards

  • Watch for 15 minutes: Weapon Sticker - Broadcast Mask
  • Watch for 30 minutes: Weapon Sticker - On Target
  • Watch for 1 hour: Profile Emblem - On Target
  • Watch for 2 hours: Profile Background - Broadcast Disrupt
  • Watch for 3 hours: Magnum Weapon Style - Transmit Engine
  • Gift 1 sub: Bully Weapon Style - Transmit Engine

New Platform Cosmetics

Finally, to celebrate the launch of Season 2, players on PlayStation 5, Steam, and XBOX Series X|S can collect a new pack of unique cosmetics.

PlayStation 5

Marathon Digital Bundle for PlayStation®Plus - Null Tempest

  • Vandal Runner Shell Style - Null Tempest
  • Overrun AR Weapon Style - Null Tempest
  • Profile Background - Tempest Factor
  • Weapon Charm - TEMPESTworm

How to claim: This bundle can be claimed by PlayStation ® Plus subscribers by visiting the PlayStation® Store. Items are granted on the next login after claiming

Steam

Marathon Steam Bundle - Spectral Mist

  • Assassin Runner Shell Style - Spectral Mist
  • Overrun AR Weapon Style - Spectral Mist
  • Profile Background - Internal Affair
  • Weapon Charm - MISTworm

How to claim: This bundle can be claimed in the Steam store from the “Additional Content” section on the main product page. Items are granted on the next login after claiming.

XBOX Series X|S

Marathon Bundle - Emerald Impact

  • Destroyer Runner Shell Style - Emeral Impact
  • Overrun AR Weapon Style - Emeral Impact
  • Profile Background - Impact Orientation
  • Weapon Charm – IMPACTworm

How to claim: This bundle can be claimed in the XBOX store under “Add-Ons” on the main product page. Items are granted on the next login after claiming.

All of these rewards are in addition to the platform weapon charms released at launch. Learn more here.

Season 2 Begins June 2

Just one day left in Season 1 and one more sleep until Season 2 begins. Go put on all of your golds and equip those guns you have been saving before the season ends. Tomorrow there will be new challenges and fresh rewards.

Don’t be afraid of the dark.

 

*Gameplay requires internet access (sold separately)
enUs.bodyText: Open Play Week Begins June 2 At the start of Season 2, we’re opening the gates of Marathon so everyone can experience the new content and the many improvements we have made throughout Season 1. This will be a great time to invite your friends to crew up or check the game out for yourself, as everyone will be starting out on a fresh page and learning what’s new together. During the Open Play Week * from June 2 to June 9 , you can experience everything the new season has to offer, including the new Night Marsh zone, a new Runner shell, new gear, and more. Anyone who chooses to purchase will keep all items and progression after the Open Play Week ends. As a reminder, for those who already own Marathon, Season 2 gameplay content will be available for no additional cost. Dive into what’s new in Season 2: Season 2 Developer Insights Video Welcome to Season 2 Article Season 2 Sandbox Changes Article Season 2 Cinematic Trailer New Experimental Mode: Sponsored Survival The start of Season 2 also brings a new experimental mode to Marathon from June 2 to June 9. As mentioned in our recent post , we want to explore more survival experiences for different moods, like if you want to go full sweat or would rather lean back and chill. This new experimental mode lands somewhere in between, and is a more PVP-lite and low risk way to play Marathon than typical runs. In Sponsored Survival , a single crew will infil onto the new Night Marsh zone. They’ll start as the only players on the map and can explore, loot, and blast their flashlights without fear of repercussion... from PVP enemies anyways. The UESC will still be there in full force, as will some new enemies that lurk in the shadows. After a certain period of time, backfilled Rooks can enter the map and begin their own scavenging excursions. Perhaps they’ll remain lone wolves, or maybe they’ll team up with each other or the sole crew on the map—everyone's goal is survival, and how you get there is up to you. In this mode, your one and only ticket off the map is to survive to the final exfil. Each match has an 18-minute run timer, and once it hits zero, the final exfil will spawn. Everyone will have a limited amount of time to book it over and prepare to make their exit. Once there, the decision is yours: Will you negotiate a temporary truce and exfil as one, or turn your weapons on each other in the dead of night? This asymmetrical mode changes the dynamics of a run, taking the heat off of the lone crew for the first few minutes of the match, while also keeping some of the fun, social unpredictability that Rooks bring to runs. Crews are required to bring a Sponsored Kit loadout to play, which keeps the stakes low for the crew, while also giving Rooks the peace of mind of knowing there’s no fully kitted-out teams on the map. When you play as the crew, you can use queue fill to find teammates, or disable it to play as a duo or solo. While this mode is running, other Rook queues will be disabled and all Rooks will filter into Sponsored Survival. Like our previous experimental modes—Duos Queue and Sponsored Queue—Sponsored Survival gives us a chance to test the waters with a different way to play Marathon. Each of these experiments has the potential to inform future experiments, core ways to play, or permanent additions to the game. We’ll also be testing a PVE mode in the latter half of Season 2, with more experiments to come later, such as perhaps a more purely PVP-focused mode. Shoutout to everyone who’s participated in the experimental queues so far, and please keep your thoughts and feedback coming. Announcing Season 2 Twitch Drops Season 2 also brings a new set of Twitch Drops, available from June 2 at 10am PT to June 9 at 10am PT. There are five new rewards available for tuning in to anyone streaming in the Marathon category, and one reward available for gifting a sub. Twitch Drop Rewards Watch for 15 minutes: Weapon Sticker - Broadcast Mask Watch for 30 minutes : Weapon Sticker - On Target Watch for 1 hour : Profile Emblem - On Target Watch for 2 hours : Profile Background - Broadcast Disrupt Watch for 3 hours : Magnum Weapon Style - Transmit Engine Gift 1 sub: Bully Weapon Style - Transmit Engine New Platform Cosmetics Finally, to celebrate the launch of Season 2, players on PlayStation 5, Steam, and XBOX Series X|S can collect a new pack of unique cosmetics. PlayStation 5 Marathon Digital Bundle for PlayStation®Plus - Null Tempest Vandal Runner Shell Style - Null Tempest Overrun AR Weapon Style - Null Tempest Profile Background - Tempest Factor Weapon Charm - TEMPESTworm How to claim: This bundle can be claimed by PlayStation ® Plus subscribers by visiting the PlayStation® Store. Items are granted on the next login after claiming Steam Marathon Steam Bundle - Spectral Mist Assassin Runner Shell Style - Spectral Mist Overrun AR Weapon Style - Spectral Mist Profile Background - Internal Affair Weapon Charm - MISTworm How to claim: This bundle can be claimed in the Steam store from the “Additional Content” section on the main product page. Items are granted on the next login after claiming. XBOX Series X|S Marathon Bundle - Emerald Impact Destroyer Runner Shell Style - Emeral Impact Overrun AR Weapon Style - Emeral Impact Profile Background - Impact Orientation Weapon Charm – IMPACTworm How to claim: This bundle can be claimed in the XBOX store under “Add-Ons” on the main product page. Items are granted on the next login after claiming. All of these rewards are in addition to the platform weapon charms released at launch. Learn more here . Season 2 Begins June 2 Just one day left in Season 1 and one more sleep until Season 2 begins. Go put on all of your golds and equip those guns you have been saving before the season ends. Tomorrow there will be new challenges and fresh rewards. Don’t be afraid of the dark. *Gameplay requires internet access (sold separately)

zhChs.title: 公开试玩周6月3日 - 6月10日
zhChs.subtitle: 整整一周时间，体验完整游戏内容，包括第2赛季以及全新实验模式
zhChs.author: Marathon Dev Team
zhChs.bodyHtml:
公开试玩周于6月3日开启

在第2赛季开始时，我们将向所有人敞开《失落星船：马拉松》的大门，让所有人都能体验全新内容，以及我们在第1赛季期间所做的诸多改进。所有人都将从同一起跑线出发，一同探索全新内容。如果你想邀请好友一起组队游玩或是独自体验，现在就是最好时机。

在6月3日至6月10日公开试玩周*期间，你可以体验新赛季的全部内容，包括新夜间沼泽区域、新疾行者外壳、新装备等。选择购买游戏的玩家将在公开试玩周结束后保留所有物品和游戏进度。如果你已拥有《失落星船：马拉松》，那么无需额外付费即可体验第2赛季的游戏内容。

深入了解第2赛季的新内容：

  • 第2赛季开发者见解视频
  • 欢迎来到第2赛季博文
  • 第2赛季沙盒改动博文
  • 第2赛季过场动画预告片

全新实验模式：赞助生存

随着第2赛季开启，《失落星船：马拉松》也将在6月3日至6月10日期间推出全新的实验模式。正如我们近期在博文中提到的，无论你是想全力以赴，还是只想打把休闲局，我们希望能为不同的游玩心境探索多样的生存体验。而这个全新的实验模式，正介于两者之间。相比常规疾行，它可以让你用PVP程度更轻、风险更低的玩法体验《失落星船：马拉松》。

在赞助生存中，一支团队将渗透进入全新的夜间沼泽区域。作为地图上唯一的玩家团队，他们可以自由探索、搜刮物资，甚至肆无忌惮地打开手电筒，而不用去担心遭到袭击……至少不用担心来自敌对玩家的攻击。当然，UESC的部队依旧会全副武装驻守此地，同时还有一些潜伏在黑暗中的新敌人等待着你。

经过一段时间后，补位的鲁克也会进入地图，开始他们各自的搜刮行动。他们也许会选择独自行动，也可能会彼此合作，甚至与地图上的唯一一支团队联手。毕竟，所有人的目标都是活下去。至于通过何种手段达成目的，全由你自己决定。

在这个模式中，你离开地图的唯一方式就是撑到最终撤离。每场比赛的疾行时间限制为18分钟，当计时归零时，最终撤离点就会出现。所有人都必须在有限的时间内迅速赶往撤离区域，并做好离开的准备。到达之后，选择权也交到了你手中：究竟是达成临时休战、一起撤离，还是在暗夜之中，将枪口对准彼此？

这个非对称模式为整场疾行的节奏带来了变化。在比赛开始后的前几分钟，场上唯一的团队不会承受过多的敌对压力。同时也保留了鲁克加入对局所带来的有趣变数。团队必须使用赞助包配装才能进入该模式。这不仅降低了团队的损失风险，也能让鲁克放心游玩，毕竟地图上不会出现武装到牙齿的强力队伍。

当你组队游玩时，可以开启团队补位寻找队友，也可以关闭该功能，以单排或双排游玩。在该模式开放期间，其他鲁克队列都将暂时关闭，所有鲁克都会自动进入赞助生存模式。

和此前的实验模式（双人队列和赞助队列）一样，赞助生存模式让我们有机会测试《失落星船：马拉松》的另一种游玩方式。而每一项实验都有可能影响未来的实验玩法、核心玩法，甚至作为永久模式加入游戏。我们还将在第2赛季后半段测试一个PVE模式，未来也会继续推出更多实验玩法，例如更偏向纯PVP的模式。

也感谢所有热心参与实验队列测试的玩家，请继续和我们分享你的想法与反馈。

第2赛季Twitch掉落

第2赛季还将带来一组全新的Twitch掉落，活动时间为北京时间6月3日凌晨1点至6月10日凌晨1点。观看《失落星船：马拉松》分类下的任意直播，即可获得五项全新奖励，赠送订阅还可额外获得一项奖励。

Twitch掉落奖励

  • 观看15分钟：武器贴纸 - 广播面具
  • 观看30分钟：武器贴纸 - 锁定目标
  • 观看1小时：档案徽标 - 锁定目标
  • 观看2小时：档案背景 - 广播干扰
  • 观看3小时：马格南武器风格 - 传输引擎
  • 赠送订阅：恶霸武器风格 - 传输引擎

全新平台专属装饰

最后，为庆祝第2赛季上线，PlayStation 5、Steam与XBOX Series X|S的玩家还可领取一套全新的独特装饰奖励。

PlayStation 5

PlayStation®Plus《失落星船：马拉松》数字组合包——零域风暴

  • 暴徒疾行者外壳风格 - 零域风暴
  • 奔袭突击步枪武器风格 - 零域风暴
  • 档案背景 - 风暴系数
  • 武器挂件 - 风暴虫

领取方式：PlayStation®Plus订阅用户可前往PlayStation® Store领取该组合包。领取后，下次登录游戏即可获得物品。

Steam

《失落星船：马拉松》Steam组合包——幽灵迷雾

  • 刺客疾行者外壳风格 - 幽灵迷雾
  • 奔袭突击步枪武器风格 - 幽灵迷雾
  • 档案背景 - 内部事务
  • 武器挂件 - 雾虫

领取方式：可在Steam商店主产品页面中的“附加内容”领取该组合包。领取后，下次登录游戏即可获得物品。

Xbox Series X|S

《失落星船：马拉松》组合包——翡翠冲击

  • 毁灭者疾行者外壳风格 - 翡翠冲击
  • 奔袭突击步枪武器风格 - 翡翠冲击
  • 档案背景 - 冲击导向
  • 武器挂件 – 冲击虫

领取方式：可在XBOX商店主产品页面中的“附加内容”领取该组合包。领取后，下次登录游戏即可获得物品。

以上所有奖励，均为首发时推出的平台专属武器挂件之外的额外内容。在此处了解更多。

第2赛季将于6月3日正式开启

距离第1赛季结束只剩最后一天了。一夜安睡后，第2赛季就将正式开启。趁此收官之际，穿上最好的装备，拿上压箱底的武器再来一场疾行吧。因为明天开始，将会有全新的挑战与奖励等待着你。

不要畏惧黑暗。

 

*游戏需要联网，且主机平台需付费订阅（另行购买）

enUs.title: Season 1 Comes To A Close…
enUs.subtitle: …Time to see how you all did.
enUs.author: Marathon Dev Team
enUs.bodyHtml:
It’s been three months. Three months of carving your own path across Tau Ceti IV, scavenging supplies, taking out the opposition, and securing new contracts to grow your abilities to their limits. Within that time, you all did some amazing things.

Here’s a look at how every Runner made your mark this season.

Closing Out This Season

As we reach the end of this season, there’s a lot to look forward to. You’ll be securing your loot rewards for your Runner Level you worked hard to build. As mentioned in our Season 2 article, everyone will earn Sponsored Kits based on your Runner Level at the end of this season, unlockable in the Codex once Season 2 starts.

Reach Runner Level 10: Unlock Enhanced CyberAcme Sponsored Kit

  • Enhanced Rarity D54 Battle Pistol
  • Light Ammo
  • 3 Smoke Grenades
  • 2 Random Enhanced Rarity Runner Cores
  • 1 Random Enhanced Rarity Head Implant
  • 1 Random Enhanced Rarity Torso Implant
  • 1 Random Enhanced Rarity Leg Implant
  • 1 Enhanced Rarity Shield Implant
  • 1 Enhanced Rarity Base Backpack
  • 1 Stack of Enhanced Shield Charges
  • 1 Stack of Enhanced Patch Kits
  • 1 Deluxe Shield Charge
  • 1 Deluxe Patch Kit

Reach Runner Level 25: Unlock Enhanced CyberAcme Sponsored Kit

  • You’ll earn another copy of the above Enhanced CyberAcme Sponsored Kit if you achieved Runner Level 25.

Reach Runner Level 50: Deluxe CyberAcme Sponsored Kit

  • Deluxe Rarity Twin Tap
  • Heavy Ammo
  • 3 Smoke Grenades
  • 2 Random Deluxe Rarity Runner Cores
  • 1 Random Deluxe Rarity Head Implant
  • 1 Random Deluxe Rarity Torso Implant
  • 1 Random Deluxe Rarity Leg Implant
  • 1 Deluxe Rarity Shield Implant
  • 1 Deluxe Rarity Base Backpack
  • 2 Stacks of Enhanced Shield Charges
  • 2 Stacks of Enhanced Patch Kits
  • 2 Deluxe Shield Charges
  • 2 Deluxe Patch Kits

Reach Runner Level 75: Superior CyberAcme Sponsored Kit

  • Superior Rarity M77
  • Light Ammo
  • 3 Smoke Grenades
  • 2 Random Superior Rarity Runner Cores
  • 1 Random Superior Rarity Head Implant
  • 1 Random Superior Rarity Torso Implant
  • 1 Random Superior Rarity Leg Implant
  • 1 Superior Rarity Shield Implant
  • 1 Superior Rarity Base Backpack
  • 2 Stacks of Enhanced Shield Charges
  • 2 Stacks of Enhanced Patch Kits
  • 6 Deluxe Shield Charges
  • 6 Deluxe Patch Kits
  • 1 Panacea Kit
  • 1 Self Revive Kit

Remember that these kits are additive! The higher the level, the more Sponsored Kits you’ll get.

What’s Next?

Soon, the next chapter will be upon us. Season 2 starts on June 2, and we’re excited to see Runners dive into the newest content. Our dev insights goes into the latest details of what’s coming, including;

  • An updated Dire Marsh (Night) zone
  • New Sentinel Runner shell
  • Updated sandbox changes to weapons, mods, and chips
  • Our new progression system, the Cradle
  • New weapons, updated contracts, and faction progression improvements

Want to try out the new changes with some friends? Curious about what Season 2 has to offer? Join us from June 2 to June 9 for our Open Play Week! You’ll be able to try out the game without any purchase required* for that week, and any progress you make during the Open Play Week will cross over to your account if you purchase a copy of Marathon after.

 

 

*Gameplay requires internet access and paid-for subscription on console (sold separately)
enUs.bodyText: It’s been three months. Three months of carving your own path across Tau Ceti IV, scavenging supplies, taking out the opposition, and securing new contracts to grow your abilities to their limits. Within that time, you all did some amazing things. Here’s a look at how every Runner made your mark this season. Closing Out This Season As we reach the end of this season, there’s a lot to look forward to. You’ll be securing your loot rewards for your Runner Level you worked hard to build. As mentioned in our Season 2 article , everyone will earn Sponsored Kits based on your Runner Level at the end of this season, unlockable in the Codex once Season 2 starts. Reach Runner Level 50: Deluxe CyberAcme Sponsored Kit Deluxe Rarity Twin Tap Heavy Ammo 3 Smoke Grenades 2 Random Deluxe Rarity Runner Cores 1 Random Deluxe Rarity Head Implant 1 Random Deluxe Rarity Torso Implant 1 Random Deluxe Rarity Leg Implant 1 Deluxe Rarity Shield Implant 1 Deluxe Rarity Base Backpack 2 Stacks of Enhanced Shield Charges 2 Stacks of Enhanced Patch Kits 2 Deluxe Shield Charges 2 Deluxe Patch Kits Reach Runner Level 75: Superior CyberAcme Sponsored Kit Superior Rarity M77 Light Ammo 3 Smoke Grenades 2 Random Superior Rarity Runner Cores 1 Random Superior Rarity Head Implant 1 Random Superior Rarity Torso Implant 1 Random Superior Rarity Leg Implant 1 Superior Rarity Shield Implant 1 Superior Rarity Base Backpack 2 Stacks of Enhanced Shield Charges 2 Stacks of Enhanced Patch Kits 6 Deluxe Shield Charges 6 Deluxe Patch Kits 1 Panacea Kit 1 Self Revive Kit Remember that these kits are additive! enUs.title: Season 2 Combat Tuning Preview
enUs.subtitle: Prepare to be prepared.
enUs.author: Marathon Dev Team
enUs.bodyHtml:
Today, we wanted to give you a clearer look at the kind of work taking shape for Season 2’s combat sandbox. Learn more about Season 2, including Night Marsh, the new Runner shell Sentinel, and the Cradle here.

Season 2 gives us a chance to keep building on the parts of Marathon that players are already connecting with, while also making the overall combat experience clearer, more expressive, and more rewarding to learn over time.

The new season gives us a chance to take a holistic look at everything in the game beyond isolated changes It’s about how fights come together as a whole. How your weapons feel in their intended role. How your build changes the decisions you make. How new tools can help you survive when things get hectic. And how your Runner shell's kit can embody more identity from one encounter to the next.

We’re focusing on weapons, mods, buildcrafting, equipment, survival, Runner shells, implants, and the sandbox as a whole in Season 2. Across all of these areas, the goal is the same: We want your choices to feel more intentional, we want to make engaging with our deep systems easier, and we want more parts of your loadout to feel like they truly matter once you're Tau Ceti-side.

Let’s get into it.

Weapons and Sandbox

Let’s start with the loud part.

For the launch of Season 2, we are focusing on making weapons easier to use and reinforcing their intended roles. We want weapons to be clearer in their purpose and more distinct from one another when you're both using them and fighting against them.

That work includes broader updates to ammo economy, time to kill, and range identities so that close-range tools stay dangerous up close, mid-range options better hold their ground, and weapon roles are easier to understand from one fight to the next.

Season 2 also brings a couple of new weapons into that conversation:

Some longtime, keen-eyed Marathon fans may have noticed one of our new weapons might be a little familiar. Coming in Season 2, the KKV-9SD is pistol frame SMG with an integrated suppressor and is a perfect companion for fighting the terrors on Night Marsh. This means it takes pistol optic and pistol magazine mods, as well as chips. It is the fastest fire rate we've ever shipped on a weapon and excels at close range combat, specifically mowing down eager shotgun rushers. Its Prestige mod, Flechette Drum, adds sustainability by healing you when breaking a hostile's shield.

Our second weapon for Season 2 is the D54 Battle Pistol which is a fully automatic, 3-round burst handgun. The Battle Pistol is designed to flex between Pistol and Magnum archetypes and to have advantage in 1v1 duels. It is an excellent companion when used with other hard-hitting archetypes—don't waste time reloading your sniper or shotgun, swap to the D54 Battle Pistol and you're good to go. Its Prestige mod, the Daredevil Stock, takes advantage of the new folding stock mod archetype: while folded, enemy defeats builds up to 2 stacks, and when unfolded, the D54 Battle Pistol's fire is converted into a super burst of 9 rounds, which can be fired as many times as you have stacks. More on the new folding stock below.

The broader hope here is simple. We want weapon choices to feel more deliberate, and we want you to be able to feel what makes a weapon special in a way that breaks down friction and lets you dive into the fight you most want to take.

So while we won’t go through every weapon change here (look out for the full patch notes on release), let’s start with the monster in the room: Misriah 2442. While it’s easy to say the Misriah rose up in the wake of the WSTR changes, the truth is it was always a strong and popular option, and it ended up on our radar long before its continued dominance.

The biggest change we’re making here isn’t to the Misriah itself; rather, we are removing the rate of fire bonuses from Slick Mag magazine mods altogether. While it was an interesting experiment since the Misriah is the only weapon to take those mods, it isn’t sustainable. The idea was Misriah’s main weakness was its slow rate of fire, allowing players to counter Misriah by correctly spacing and attacking in between shots. Slick Mag presented an opportunity to shore up that weakness, but it’s clear it was pushing the weapon and its Prestige mod too far out of band.

So to that end, we’ve made the following suite of changes:

Misriah 2442

  • Increased pellet spread in hip fire by 10%.
  • Increased pellet spread while aiming down sights by 5%.
  • Increased base rate of fire from 58RPM to 72RPM.
    • This is to compensate for the removal of increased fire rate stats previously granted by Slick Mag magazine mod family.
  • Rebased magazine stat so now every mag will result in at least +1 round in the magazine.
  • Base magazine size remains at 4 and max magazine size remains 10.
  • Reduced base damage falloff start from 11 meters to 10 meters.
  • Increased max damage falloff start from 15 meters to 16 meters.

Full-Auto Selector Prestige Mod

This mod had static gameplay and buildcrafting experiences, so to improve these we've made the following changes:

  • Rate of fire increase granted from Full-Auto Selector reduced from 150RPM to 90RPM.
  • Added +50 ADS Speed and +50 ADS Accuracy.
  • While firing from the hip, Misriah will fire full auto, have an additional 10% pellet spread increase, and massively increased Stability.
  • While aiming down sights, Misriah has massively increased range and much tighter pellet spread but does not fire full auto.

We believe these changes allow for more interesting spatial plays with the Prestige Mod, utilizing the different firing modes based on proximity to enemies while also tying in more buildcrafting potential—you can focus on increasing the potency of either the hip fire mode or the ADS mode.

Let’s talk about the other end of the spectrum. While the Misriah is a popular weapon, the Overrun and the CE Tactical Sidearm were less popular options.

First up on the Overrun, we wanted to keep it simple to start because this weapon is only a couple of value changes away from <ahem> running over the other rifles. But we agree it still needs a little bit of juice. So for Season 2’s launch we made the following changes:

Overrun

  • Reduced horizontal and vertical recoil for base weapon, resulting in more control at lower rarity tiers:
    • Horizontal recoil reduced by ~50%.
    • Vertical recoil reduced by ~12%.

The high-level Superior Overrun is still performing pretty admirably against other rifles, so our focus was to make running a lower-tier Overrun in a budget build or in a Sponsored Kit less painful. Go ahead, enjoy those CyAc Sponsored Kits.

For the CE Tactical Sidearm, this weapon was always tuned in a way that if you built into it, and were hitting headshots you’d find plenty of success—but it’s clear we weren’t hitting that mark. Even at fully buildcrafted, the CE Tactical Sidearm is just not attractive enough to secure a warm seat in your Vault. In Season 2 you can expect the following changes:

CE Tactical Sidearm

  • Increased the speed at which the weapon's recoil settles when firing by 15%.
  • Reduced ADS accuracy error multiplier by ~50%.
  • Rebased aim assist and magnetism min and max falloff distances:
    • Aim Assist
      • Minimum distance increased from 11 meters to 14 meters at base range stat and from 14 meters to 22 meters at maximum range stat.
      • Maximum distance increased from 22 meters to 25 meters at base range stat and from 21 meters to 29 meters at maximum range stat.
    • Magnetism
      • Minimum distance increased from 15 meters to 18 meters at base range stat and from 18.6 meters to 29.6 meters at maximum range stat.
      • Maximum distance increased from 25 meters to 26.2 meters at base range stat and from 24.8 meters to 39 meters at maximum range stat.
  • Rebased aim assist cone size stats:
    • Increased aim assist cone size at base aim assist stat by 33%.
    • Increased aim assist cone size at max aim assist stat by 16%.
    • Rebased hip fire and ADS projectile accuracy stats to compensate.
      • In general, this means an increase in projectile error all up to account for the increased aim assist cone size.
  • Reduced crouch accuracy bonus at max crouch accuracy stat by 117%.
  • Reduced moving accuracy bonus at minimum moving accuracy stat by 22%.
  • Increased moving accuracy bonus at maximum moving accuracy stat by 50%.

There’s a handful of more weapon-specific tunings happening at the launch of Season 2, but these are some of the big headliners. Expect more changes in the full patches on June 2!

Mods and Buildcrafting

A lot of combat starts before the first shot. Careful preparation and buildcrafting are key to survival on Tau Ceti IV. Season 2 was a chance to reflect on a lot of the mod stories for weapons and think about how we can make choices more meaningful and how mods can do more than just push stats up or down.

For Season 2, we're investing heavily in a few key areas for mods. First, we're adding two new families of mods and eight new families of chips. We also took heavy balance passes on every magazine and every optic mod. There's a lot to dive into; far more than we could detail in this article, so look for the full scope of every change coming to mods in the full patch notes.

Let's talk about the two new mod families. First up we have Folding Stocks:

Folding stocks are a new mod family that will first be present on the new KKV and D54 Battle Pistol weapons. They are similar to Shield Mods for the LMGs in that they provide a unique gameplay altering action.

When equipped, folding stocks are initially collapsed, and in this mode, they provide boosts to stats like hip fire accuracy and movement speed with that weapon. You can then press your alternate weapon action input to unfold the stock, trading the hip fire focused bonuses for boosts to ADS accuracy and range. We really love how these mods provide options for different playstyles on a single weapon and the ability to change strategies on the fly during combat.

Second, we're adding an entirely new archetype of optics mods, the Darksight Scopes. Naturally, with the debut of Night Marsh, we want to make sure you have all the tools necessary for precision execution in a dark and dangerous place.

Every archetype of weapon will receive their own Darksight Scope, and they come in two different rarities: Deluxe and Superior. All Darksight scopes will operate similarly. They all are a full screen vignette when aiming down sights—similar to how sniper optics already work—but for every weapon. Secondly, while ADS, you can press the toggle optic input to activate the night vision effect and light up the world around them. The night vision effect is wider and covers more distance with the Superior version. Our goal is for their unique gameplay to be helpful not only on Night Marsh, but across the rest of the game as well.

Weapon chips are the backbone of weapon buildcrafting. They are the software upgrades that make your weapon do things that seem almost impossible. And on the topic of chips, we do have to do a bit of housekeeping to do. With Season 2, we are looking to rotate out a couple of chip families that were not meeting the bar for quality and performance—on both ends of the spectrum:

  • Stack Overflow
    • Rotated all rarities out of the loot pool for Season 2.
  • Optimal Prime
    • Rotated all rarities out of the loot pool for Season 2.

Stack Overflow was overperforming on certain weapons and in other cases was performing wildly below the bar. So we’re taking this one back to the lab for now. Optimal Prime, while potentially interesting from a buildcrafting standpoint, just did not find the niche we were hoping for, so that too will take a seat on the bench.

But fear not, eight new chip families are rotating in for their time in the sun. We won’t spoil everything here but here’s a couple of highlights:

  • Alarmist (all rarities)
    • After aiming down sights for a brief duration, projectile impacts create a terrifying scan drone alert.
      • We heard you liked Ornithologist but we agree it’s weird to hear birds on Cryo. Maybe this will help?
  • Scrapyard (all rarities)
    • Defeating hostiles with this weapon has a chance to spawn depleted health items or standard materials.
  • Brain Freeze (Superior rarity only)
    • Precision downs or kills with this weapon create a small explosion that spreads Frost to nearby hostiles.

But already existing chips also had some changes too. Again, there’s too many to go through here but here’s a couple of previews:

  • Bounty Hunter
    • Increased the amount of credits rewarded per UESC kill:
      • Standard: 10 credits increased to 25 credits.
      • Enhanced: 15 credits increased to 50 credits.
      • Deluxe: 20 credits increased to 75 credits.
      • Superior: 25 credits increased to 100 credits.
    • Fixed an issue where Bounty Hunter wasn't awarding credits upon killing UESC scan drones.
  • Cloudborn
    • Redesigned perk benefits entirely:
    • While in smoke, this weapon passively has increased stability, accuracy, handling, and movement speed.
      • Previously the benefit was overflowing the magazine on reloading while in smoke.

Lastly for mods, we wanted to touch on optics and magazine mods. Every optic and magazine mod in the game had a full stat balance pass. These changes were largely to sand down some outliers while also trying to make more interesting choices for all the weapons that use any given family of optic or magazine. But there are a couple of big, important changes to note:

  • Starting in Season 2, there will be no thermal optic option for snipers only.
    • Even as we brought in their range, snipers with thermals were just overperforming. We don’t view this as a permanent benching, but we are taking the time to cook on some longer gestating changes for how thermals work all up and until that time, we decided it was better to pull this ripcord now.
  • Starting in Season 2, one Superior magazine mod in every family will come with the new Vector Rounds perk on it by default.
    • Vector Rounds is a new perk designed as an information tool that can help you mark space, track enemy movement, and create visibility in dark corners.
    • After aiming down sights for a short duration, the next projectile impact will create a revealing pulse, similar to the Darksight effect. Enemies caught in this detonation will be highlighted for a brief duration.

We’re extremely eager to see how buildcrafting is shaken up in Season 2. For the complete breakdown, the full patch notes will have you covered.

Equipment and Survival

Weapons aren't the only tools at your disposal to survive out there during a run. Equipment plays a vital role in the combat sandbox and is often what players use to turn the tide of a fight.

... though perhaps some equipment items were a little too strong in Season 1. Before we get into the three new pieces of equipment you can expect to unleash next season, we want to take the opportunity to outline a few changes coming to equipment at the start of Season 2.

  • Grenade ready animations extended by 0.2 seconds

The ready animation plays when a grenade is pulled out and primed. The throw (after the button has been released) is unchanged. We feel this change preserves the responsiveness and joy of throwing a grenade, but has a measurable effect on how many grenades can be thrown in rapid succession. The extra time also allowed us to polish the ready animations and make them a little more expressive.

  • Frag Grenade
    • Reduced max damage by 15% (135 → 115).
  • Heat Grenade
    • Reduced damage by 50% (40 → 20).
  •  Signal Jammer
    • Visual and detection jamming will temporarily flicker off when a player performs actions, uses abilities, shoots their weapon, or takes damage.
    • No longer blocks detection by Early Warning System (Prestige Recon Core).
    • No longer blocks detection by Vital Intel (Stryder M1T Prestige Optic Mod).

Signal Jammers in Season 1 felt like a default requirement for any engagement because they offered a flat, guaranteed advantage whether you were repositioning for an ambush or sprinting directly into combat. We hope this change will make Signal Jammers reward tactical movement and stealth while being less effective during a firefight.

With that housekeeping out of the way, let’s talk about the new stuff.

First up, we have the Frost Mine. The Frost Mine is a placeable piece of equipment, similar to a Claymore. It detonates when an enemy is nearby and instead of a high damage conical blast like the Claymore, it explodes in a spherical blast dealing moderate damage but also spreading the Frost debuff to any enemy caught in it. Stay warm out there.

Second, we have the Vector Grenade. This grenade’s primary purpose is not damage, but rather information. When a Vector Grenade detonates, it creates a field of revealing pulses that linger in the world for a short time, highlighting spaces in a large area and any enemies inside its radius. This grenade is an essential companion in Night Marsh and in other hard-to-see places.

Lastly, we’re adding Signal Flares in three colors. Signal flares come in red, green, and blue varieties, and they each have a different lens flare shape. These don’t focus on damage but linger in the world providing some amount of light. We view these as communication tools for non-verbal Runners. Maybe in the heat of a fight, green flares are thrown and a new alliance is formed? Maybe you come across a blue flare that marks a piece of loot on the ground left by a benevolent Runner? The choice is in yours hands.

Runners and Implants

Your biosynthetic shell is the foundation that defines your playstyle. In Season 2, we’re taking the first step of expanding our roster to give you more ways to explore and experience our perilous, alien world.

Meet Sentinel. This shell is a defensive specialist, made for carving out pockets of space within the battlefield and securing them with clever traps... and lethal force.

Sentinel’s kit is built around area control and adaptability under pressure. It brings a few fresh new tools to the battlefield:

  • Defender System (Prime Ability):
    • Deploy a defensive platform that harmlessly neutralizes incoming explosives. You and your allies also gain increased weapon stability and reload speed when standing near the device. And as always, bear in mind that it’s got a limited number of projectiles to fire! Be sure to keep a steady eye out.
  • Snare Mine (Tactical Ability):
    • Toss a proximity-triggered mine which detonates into several Immobilizing submunitions when enemies get too close.
  • Prey Tracker (Trait Ability):
    • Activate motion tracking systems in your HUD that allow you to see moving enemies on radar within a conical zone in front of you.
  • Castle Doctrine (Passive):
    • You ready and reload SMGs, Pistols, and Shotguns more quickly based on the number of nearby hostiles. When you take splash damage, your Hardware, Firewall, and Self-Repair Speed are increased for a short time.

Use your Prey Tracker to gather real-time information about enemy positions as they move, allowing you to set up the perfect ambush or hunt down a fleeing Assassin. Use your Snare Mines to stop a shotgun-rushing Destroyer in their tracks, or toss one under a UESC dropship for some immediate crowd control. When things get ugly, bunker down by deploying a Defender System to neutralize an incoming Rocket Drone missile barrage and whip out your KKV or Misriah to give a warm welcome to any unwanted visitors.

We’re also adding a full suite of 10 Sentinel-specific Runner cores across the various rarity tiers. Here’s a small taste:

  • Reversal (Enhanced): Defeating an Immobilized target heals you over time.
    • Great for adding some sustain to your loadout if you’re low on medical consumables and don’t have a Triage in your crew.
  • Wellness Beacon (Deluxe): While standing near your Defender System, you and your allies use medical items more quickly.
    • Useful for more support-focused playstyles where you want to keep your allies in the fight longer.
  • Eminent Domain (Prestige): Runner grenades neutralized by your Defender System fall to the floor and become lootable.
    • The ultimate tool for countering throwable spam–literally throw your opponent’s grenades back at them.

We can’t wait to see how Sentinel shakes up the meta and what kinds of devious (or hilarious) plays you get up to.

Implants

Now let’s talk about implants.

Initially, our plan was to give cores and implants unique identities by focusing cores on powerful Runner-specific perks and implants on interesting packages of stat buffs. Our early playtesting led us to add random perks to implants in addition to the stats to help create more variety in the buildcrafting game. These proved very effective at expanding the depth of different playstyles available and quickly became the primary element driving choice around implants.

However, our implementation in Season 1 led to a number of problems. The system is challenging to use because the item names reflect the stat package and not what an implant’s perk is, information about what that perk does is buried in the tooltip, and you can’t look at an item in a container or on the floor and intuitively understand what exactly it does.

In Season 2, we’re taking significant steps to address these issues by reworking how implants are structured. Implants will be primarily focused on perks, with Runner stats being a secondary element. Every implant now grants a single fixed perk, which is reflected directly in the item name, and has a fixed stat package. As an example, you’ll be able to loot a Tox Injectors head implant which will give bonuses to your Firewall, Heat Capacity and Fall Resistance, or a Triage Cloaking Device torso implant which grants Revive Speed, Tactical Recovery, and Hardware.

With this rework, you’ll be able to collect all the same perks you could in Season 1 (even the unique perks from Prestige-tier implants, if you’re lucky!) plus eight brand-new ones we’ve added in Season 2, such as Going Dark which makes you invisible while using healing consumables. Certain implants will only drop at higher rarities, which you’ll need to delve into more challenging zones to find... or you can just acquire them from your fellow Runners.

One last note is that this is not the final form of implants. At-a-glance readability of the implant icons themselves the next thing on our radar, and we’re cooking up some more changes here that we’re excited to roll out in the future.

Finally, we wanted to touch briefly on the rest of our changes for Runner shells.

Our focus this season has largely been on bugfixing and quality-of-life updates, such as improvements to the mantle system to make climbing up rough terrain more consistent, making movement smoother and reducing instances of desync, and addressing deathbox spawning to prevent them from ending up in inaccessible areas. One notable tuning change is that we’re reducing how much the Prime and Tactical Recovery stats accelerate the cooldowns of runner abilities at 100 stat, to make sure that we’re preserving opportunities for counterplay in high-level engagements.

Be on the lookout for our full patch notes page for the complete details on the changes and additional developer notes.

Out With A Bang

That’s a quick rundown of what’s to come with Season 2, so you can be better prepared dive right in on June 2nd. Reminder that there’s an Open Play Week from June 2 to June 9, where the full game will be available on Steam, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S.

Some of it is about refining the sandbox. Some of it is about adding new gear and new options. Some of it is about making the dark more playable while keeping its danger alive and well. And, some of it is about making sure that when you open your loadout, the choices in front of you feel thoughtful, useful, and worth exploring.

Our hope is that these changes make combat feel sharper, build choices feel more expressive, and every run feels like it gives you a little more room to learn, adapt, and find a style that clicks for you.

As these changes get into your hands, your feedback is going to matter a lot. We’ll be paying close attention to the places you already talk to us—especially our official Discord, Steam discussions, Reddit, and our MarathonDevTeam social channels on Twitter and Bluesky. We want to hear what is landing, what might feel off, what surprises you, and what is not doing the job it should.

Happy hunting.

While it was an interesting experiment since the Misriah is the only weapon to take those mods, it isn’t sustainable. The idea was Misriah’s main weakness was its slow rate of fire, allowing players to counter Misriah by correctly spacing and attacking in between shots. Slick Mag presented an opportunity to shore up that weakness, but it’s clear it was pushing the weapon and its Prestige mod too far out of band. So to that end, we’ve made the following suite of changes: Misriah 2442 Increased pellet spread in hip fire by 10%. Increased pellet spread while aiming down sights by 5%. Increased base rate of fire from 58RPM to 72RPM. This is to compensate for the removal of increased fire rate stats previously granted by Slick Mag magazine mod family. Rebased magazine stat so now every mag will result in at least +1 round in the magazine. Base magazine size remains at 4 and max magazine size remains 10. Reduced base damage falloff start from 11 meters to 10 meters. Increased max damage falloff start from 15 meters to 16 meters. Full-Auto Selector Prestige Mod This mod had static gameplay and buildcrafting experiences, so to improve these we've made the following changes: Rate of fire increase granted from Full-Auto Selector reduced from 150RPM to 90RPM. Added +50 ADS Speed and +50 ADS Accuracy. While firing from the hip, Misriah will fire full auto, have an additional 10% pellet spread increase, and massively increased Stability. While aiming down sights, Misriah has massively increased range and much tighter pellet spread but does not fire full auto. We believe these changes allow for more interesting spatial plays with the Prestige Mod, utilizing the different firing modes based on proximity to enemies while also tying in more buildcrafting potential—you can focus on increasing the potency of either the hip fire mode or the ADS mode. Let’s talk about the other end of the spectrum. While the Misriah is a popular weapon, the Overrun and the CE Tactical Sidearm were less popular options. First up on the Overrun, we wanted to keep it simple to start because this weapon is only a couple of value changes away from <ahem > running over the other rifles. But we agree it still needs a little bit of juice. So for Season 2’s launch we made the following changes: Overrun Reduced horizontal and vertical recoil for base weapon, resulting in more control at lower rarity tiers: Horizontal recoil reduced by ~50%. Vertical recoil reduced by ~12%. The high-level Superior Overrun is still performing pretty admirably against other rifles, so our focus was to make running a lower-tier Overrun in a budget build or in a Sponsored Kit less painful. Go ahead, enjoy those CyAc Sponsored Kits. For the CE Tactical Sidearm, this weapon was always tuned in a way that if you built into it, and were hitting headshots you’d find plenty of success—but it’s clear we weren’t hitting that mark. Even at fully buildcrafted, the CE Tactical Sidearm is just not attractive enough to secure a warm seat in your Vault. In Season 2 you can expect the following changes: CE Tactical Sidearm Increased the speed at which the weapon's recoil settles when firing by 15%. Reduced ADS accuracy error multiplier by ~50%. Rebased aim assist and magnetism min and max falloff distances: Aim Assist Minimum distance increased from 11 meters to 14 meters at base range stat and from 14 meters to 22 meters at maximum range stat. Maximum distance increased from 22 meters to 25 meters at base range stat and from 21 meters to 29 meters at maximum range stat. Magnetism Minimum distance increased from 15 meters to 18 meters at base range stat and from 18.6 meters to 29.6 meters at maximum range stat. Maximum distance increased from 25 meters to 26.2 meters at base range stat and from 24.8 meters to 39 meters at maximum range stat. Rebased aim assist cone size stats: Increased aim assist cone size at base aim assist stat by 33%. Increased aim assist cone size at max aim assist stat by 16%. Rebased hip fire and ADS projectile accuracy stats to compensate. In general, this means an increase in projectile error all up to account for the increased aim assist cone size. Reduced crouch accuracy bonus at max crouch accuracy stat by 117%. Reduced moving accuracy bonus at minimum moving accuracy stat by 22%. Increased moving accuracy bonus at maximum moving accuracy stat by 50%. There’s a handful of more weapon-specific tunings happening at the launch of Season 2, but these are some of the big headliners. Expect more changes in the full patches on June 2! Mods and Buildcrafting A lot of combat starts before the first shot. Careful preparation and buildcrafting are key to survival on Tau Ceti IV. Season 2 was a chance to reflect on a lot of the mod stories for weapons and think about how we can make choices more meaningful and how mods can do more than just push stats up or down. For Season 2, we're investing heavily in a few key areas for mods. First, we're adding two new families of mods and eight new families of chips. We also took heavy balance passes on every magazine and every optic mod. There's a lot to dive into; far more than we could detail in this article, so look for the full scope of every change coming to mods in the full patch notes. Let's talk about the two new mod families. First up we have Folding Stocks: Folding stocks are a new mod family that will first be present on the new KKV and D54 Battle Pistol weapons. They are similar to Shield Mods for the LMGs in that they provide a unique gameplay altering action. When equipped, folding stocks are initially collapsed, and in this mode, they provide boosts to stats like hip fire accuracy and movement speed with that weapon. You can then press your alternate weapon action input to unfold the stock, trading the hip fire focused bonuses for boosts to ADS accuracy and range. We really love how these mods provide options for different playstyles on a single weapon and the ability to change strategies on the fly during combat. Second, we're adding an entirely new archetype of optics mods, the Darksight Scopes . Naturally, with the debut of Night Marsh , we want to make sure you have all the tools necessary for precision execution in a dark and dangerous place. Every archetype of weapon will receive their own Darksight Scope, and they come in two different rarities: Deluxe and Superior. All Darksight scopes will operate similarly. They all are a full screen vignette when aiming down sights—similar to how sniper optics already work—but for every weapon. Secondly, while ADS, you can press the toggle optic input to activate the night vision effect and light up the world around them. The night vision effect is wider and covers more distance with the Superior version. Our goal is for their unique gameplay to be helpful not only on Night Marsh, but across the rest of the game as well. Weapon chips are the backbone of weapon buildcrafting. They are the software upgrades that make your weapon do things that seem almost impossible. And on the topic of chips, we do have to do a bit of housekeeping to do. With Season 2, we are looking to rotate out a couple of chip families that were not meeting the bar for quality and performance—on both ends of the spectrum: Stack Overflow Rotated all rarities out of the loot pool for Season 2. Optimal Prime Rotated all rarities out of the loot pool for Season 2. Stack Overflow was overperforming on certain weapons and in other cases was performing wildly below the bar. So we’re taking this one back to the lab for now. Optimal Prime, while potentially interesting from a buildcrafting standpoint, just did not find the niche we were hoping for, so that too will take a seat on the bench. But fear not, eight new chip families are rotating in for their time in the sun. We won’t spoil everything here but here’s a couple of highlights: Alarmist (all rarities) After aiming down sights for a brief duration, projectile impacts create a terrifying scan drone alert. We heard you liked Ornithologist but we agree it’s weird to hear birds on Cryo. Maybe this will help? Scrapyard (all rarities) Defeating hostiles with this weapon has a chance to spawn depleted health items or standard materials. Brain Freeze (Superior rarity only) Precision downs or kills with this weapon create a small explosion that spreads Frost to nearby hostiles. But already existing chips also had some changes too. Again, there’s too many to go through here but here’s a couple of previews: Bounty Hunter Increased the amount of credits rewarded per UESC kill: Standard: 10 credits increased to 25 credits. Enhanced: 15 credits increased to 50 credits. Deluxe: 20 credits increased to 75 credits. Superior: 25 credits increased to 100 credits. Fixed an issue where Bounty Hunter wasn't awarding credits upon killing UESC scan drones. Cloudborn Redesigned perk benefits entirely: While in smoke, this weapon passively has increased stability, accuracy, handling, and movement speed. Previously the benefit was overflowing the magazine on reloading while in smoke. Lastly for mods, we wanted to touch on optics and magazine mods. Every optic and magazine mod in the game had a full stat balance pass. These changes were largely to sand down some outliers while also trying to make more interesting choices for all the weapons that use any given family of optic or magazine. But there are a couple of big, important changes to note: Starting in Season 2, there will be no thermal optic option for snipers only. Even as we brought in their range, snipers with thermals were just overperforming. We don’t view this as a permanent benching, but we are taking the time to cook on some longer gestating changes for how thermals work all up and until that time, we decided it was better to pull this ripcord now. Starting in Season 2, one Superior magazine mod in every family will come with the new Vector Rounds perk on it by default. Vector Rounds is a new perk designed as an information tool that can help you mark space, track enemy movement, and create visibility in dark corners. After aiming down sights for a short duration, the next projectile impact will create a revealing pulse, similar to the Darksight effect. Enemies caught in this detonation will be highlighted for a brief duration. We’re extremely eager to see how buildcrafting is shaken up in Season 2. For the complete breakdown, the full patch notes will have you covered. Equipment and Survival Weapons aren't the only tools at your disposal to survive out there during a run. Equipment plays a vital role in the combat sandbox and is often what players use to turn the tide of a fight. ... though perhaps some equipment items were a little too strong in Season 1. Before we get into the three new pieces of equipment you can expect to unleash next season, we want to take the opportunity to outline a few changes coming to equipment at the start of Season 2. Grenade ready animations extended by 0.2 seconds The ready animation plays when a grenade is pulled out and primed. The throw (after the button has been released) is unchanged. We feel this change preserves the responsiveness and joy of throwing a grenade, but has a measurable effect on how many grenades can be thrown in rapid succession. The extra time also allowed us to polish the ready animations and make them a little more expressive. Frag Grenade Reduced max damage by 15% (135 → 115). Heat Grenade Reduced damage by 50% (40 → 20). Signal Jammer Visual and detection jamming will temporarily flicker off when a player performs actions, uses abilities, shoots their weapon, or takes damage. No longer blocks detection by Early Warning System (Prestige Recon Core). No longer blocks detection by Vital Intel (Stryder M1T Prestige Optic Mod). Signal Jammers in Season 1 felt like a default requirement for any engagement because they offered a flat, guaranteed advantage whether you were repositioning for an ambush or sprinting directly into combat. We hope this change will make Signal Jammers reward tactical movement and stealth while being less effective during a firefight. With that housekeeping out of the way, let’s talk about the new stuff. First up, we have the Frost Mine . The Frost Mine is a placeable piece of equipment, similar to a Claymore. It detonates when an enemy is nearby and instead of a high damage conical blast like the Claymore, it explodes in a spherical blast dealing moderate damage but also spreading the Frost debuff to any enemy caught in it. Stay warm out there. Second, we have the Vector Grenade . This grenade’s primary purpose is not damage, but rather information. When a Vector Grenade detonates, it creates a field of revealing pulses that linger in the world for a short time, highlighting spaces in a large area and any enemies inside its radius. This grenade is an essential companion in Night Marsh and in other hard-to-see places. Lastly, we’re adding Signal Flares in three colors. Signal flares come in red, green, and blue varieties, and they each have a different lens flare shape. These don’t focus on damage but linger in the world providing some amount of light. We view these as communication tools for non-verbal Runners. Maybe in the heat of a fight, green flares are thrown and a new alliance is formed? Maybe you come across a blue flare that marks a piece of loot on the ground left by a benevolent Runner? The choice is in yours hands. Runners and Implants Your biosynthetic shell is the foundation that defines your playstyle. In Season 2, we’re taking the first step of expanding our roster to give you more ways to explore and experience our perilous, alien world. Meet Sentinel. This shell is a defensive specialist, made for carving out pockets of space within the battlefield and securing them with clever traps... and lethal force. Sentinel’s kit is built around area control and adaptability under pressure. It brings a few fresh new tools to the battlefield: Defender System (Prime Ability): Deploy a defensive platform that harmlessly neutralizes incoming explosives. You and your allies also gain increased weapon stability and reload speed when standing near the device. And as always, bear in mind that it’s got a limited number of projectiles to fire! Be sure to keep a steady eye out. Snare Mine (Tactical Ability): Toss a proximity-triggered mine which detonates into several Immobilizing submunitions when enemies get too close. Prey Tracker (Trait Ability): Activate motion tracking systems in your HUD that allow you to see moving enemies on radar within a conical zone in front of you. Castle Doctrine (Passive): You ready and reload SMGs, Pistols, and Shotguns more quickly based on the number of nearby hostiles. When you take splash damage, your Hardware, Firewall, and Self-Repair Speed are increased for a short time. Use your Prey Tracker to gather real-time information about enemy positions as they move, allowing you to set up the perfect ambush or hunt down a fleeing Assassin. Use your Snare Mines to stop a shotgun-rushing Destroyer in their tracks, or toss one under a UESC dropship for some immediate crowd control. When things get ugly, bunker down by deploying a Defender System to neutralize an incoming Rocket Drone missile barrage and whip out your KKV or Misriah to give a warm welcome to any unwanted visitors. We’re also adding a full suite of 10 Sentinel-specific Runner cores across the various rarity tiers. Here’s a small taste: Reversal (Enhanced): Defeating an Immobilized target heals you over time. Great for adding some sustain to your loadout if you’re low on medical consumables and don’t have a Triage in your crew. Wellness Beacon (Deluxe): While standing near your Defender System, you and your allies use medical items more quickly. Useful for more support-focused playstyles where you want to keep your allies in the fight longer. Eminent Domain (Prestige): Runner grenades neutralized by your Defender System fall to the floor and become lootable. The ultimate tool for countering throwable spam–literally throw your opponent’s grenades back at them. We can’t wait to see how Sentinel shakes up the meta and what kinds of devious (or hilarious) plays you get up to. Implants Now let’s talk about implants . Initially, our plan was to give cores and implants unique identities by focusing cores on powerful Runner-specific perks and implants on interesting packages of stat buffs. Our early playtesting led us to add random perks to implants in addition to the stats to help create more variety in the buildcrafting game. These proved very effective at expanding the depth of different playstyles available and quickly became the primary element driving choice around implants. However, our implementation in Season 1 led to a number of problems. The system is challenging to use because the item names reflect the stat package and not what an implant’s perk is, information about what that perk does is buried in the tooltip, and you can’t look at an item in a container or on the floor and intuitively understand what exactly it does. In Season 2, we’re taking significant steps to address these issues by reworking how implants are structured. Implants will be primarily focused on perks, with Runner stats being a secondary element. Every implant now grants a single fixed perk, which is reflected directly in the item name, and has a fixed stat package. As an example, you’ll be able to loot a Tox Injectors head implant which will give bonuses to your Firewall, Heat Capacity and Fall Resistance, or a Triage Cloaking Device torso implant which grants Revive Speed, Tactical Recovery, and Hardware. With this rework, you’ll be able to collect all the same perks you could in Season 1 (even the unique perks from Prestige-tier implants, if you’re lucky!) plus eight brand-new ones we’ve added in Season 2, such as Going Dark which makes you invisible while using healing consumables. Certain implants will only drop at higher rarities, which you’ll need to delve into more challenging zones to find... or you can just acquire them from your fellow Runners. One last note is that this is not the final form of implants. At-a-glance readability of the implant icons themselves the next thing on our radar, and we’re cooking up some more changes here that we’re excited to roll out in the future. Finally, we wanted to touch briefly on the rest of our changes for Runner shells . Our focus this season has largely been on bugfixing and quality-of-life updates, such as improvements to the mantle system to make climbing up rough terrain more consistent, making movement smoother and reducing instances of desync, and addressing deathbox spawning to prevent them from ending up in inaccessible areas. One notable tuning change is that we’re reducing how much the Prime and Tactical Recovery stats accelerate the cooldowns of runner abilities at 100 stat, to make sure that we’re preserving opportunities for counterplay in high-level engagements. Be on the lookout for our full patch notes page for the complete details on the changes and additional developer notes. Out With A Bang That’s a quick rundown of what’s to come with Season 2, so you can be better prepared dive right in on June 2nd. Reminder that there’s an Open Play Week from June 2 to June 9, where the full game will be available on Steam, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S. Some of it is about refining the sandbox. Some of it is about adding new gear and new options. Some of it is about making the dark more playable while keeping its danger alive and well. And, some of it is about making sure that when you open your loadout, the choices in front of you feel thoughtful, useful, and worth exploring. Our hope is that these changes make combat feel sharper, build choices feel more expressive, and every run feels like it gives you a little more room to learn, adapt, and find a style that clicks for you. As these changes get into your hands, your feedback is going to matter a lot. We’ll be paying close attention to the places you already talk to us—especially our official Discord , Steam discussions , Reddit , and our MarathonDevTeam social channels on Twitter and Bluesky . We want to hear what is landing, what might feel off, what surprises you, and what is not doing the job it should. Happy hunting.

zhChs.title: 第2赛季战斗调整预览
zhChs.subtitle: 未雨绸缪，有备无患。
zhChs.author: Marathon Dev Team
zhChs.bodyHtml:
今天我们想要为大家清楚详尽地介绍一下我们正在为第2赛季的战斗沙盒进行着什么样的工作。请阅读这篇博文，了解更多关于第2赛季的内容，包括夜间沼泽地图、全新的疾行者外壳哨兵，以及摇篮系统。

第2赛季给了我们一个机会，让我们可以进一步打磨《失落星船：马拉松》中玩家们已经逐渐熟悉的部分，同时将整体战斗体验优化得更清晰、更直观，也让投入时间去学习能取得更大的回报。

新赛季让我们有机会全面地审视游戏中的所有部分，而非各自为政地进行单项修改。这个赛季的重点是如何让战斗呈现出整体性体验，你的武器要如何提供符合其定位的手感，你的配装会如何改变你所做的决策，新的工具在局面变得棘手时能如何帮助你生存，以及你的疾行者外壳的技能组在每一场战斗中要如何体现出更鲜明的特色。

在第2赛季中，我们的工作重心放在了武器、模组、配装、装备、生存、疾行者外壳、植入物，以及整体的沙盒上。对以上所有领域的调整，都将基于一个统一的标准：我们希望让你的选择感觉更有目的性，让我们的深层系统互动起来更容易，以及让你的配装在天仓五的战场中感觉真的能起到重要作用。

那么我们就一起来看看吧。

武器和沙盒

我们先说说最热闹的部分。

在第2赛季上线初期，我们的工作重心主要是让武器更易使用，并强化武器的设计定位。我们希望当大家在使用或对抗每一类武器时，能觉得这些武器的功能和作用更明晰，且彼此之间区别足够明显。

这项工作涉及广泛的调整，包括弹药经济、击杀时间以及射程特性，比如让近程武器在近距离上更致命，让中程武器能够帮助你更有效地防守阵地，以及让武器定位能够在每场比赛中更加易于理解。

第2赛季同时也会为游戏加入几款新武器：

一些眼尖的《失落星船：马拉松》老粉也许已经注意到了，我们的其中一件新武器看上去有点眼熟。在第2赛季加入的KKV-9SD是一把手枪架构的冲锋枪，自带内置抑制器，是与夜间沼泽的恐怖威胁作战的完美伴侣。也就是说这把枪可以搭配手枪瞄准镜、手枪弹匣模组，以及手枪芯片。它拥有游戏实装的所有武器中最快的射速，在近距离战斗中效果极好，特别是可以用来扫倒那些爱冲锋的霰弹枪突袭者。它的尊享模组，镖弹鼓匣，可以在你击破敌对目标的护盾时为你提供治疗，进而提升你的战场生存能力。

我们为第2赛季推出的第二件武器是一款全自动三连发手枪，D54战斗手枪。这把手枪的定位介于手枪和马格南两种泛型之间，在1对1决斗中拥有一定优势。它可以和其他威力较大的武器搭配使用，是非常优秀的随身佩枪。在紧要关头，不必浪费时间填装你的狙击枪或霰弹枪，切换到D54战斗手枪，一样能帮助你杀出一条血路。它的尊享模组，亡命者枪托，可以发挥出全新的折叠枪托模组的优势：在折叠状态下，击败敌人可以积攒最多两层效果，而在展开状态下，D54战斗手枪的射击模式则会转变为一次射出9发子弹的超级爆发连射，只要还有效果层数，便可持续射击。文章后段会详细介绍折叠枪托的用法。

我们对改动的预期十分简单和明确。我们希望让玩家对武器的选择更深思熟虑，也希望你能够毫无阻碍地感觉到每款武器的特殊之处，让你可以主动选择你想要体验的战斗。

在这里，我们不会事无巨细地介绍每一项武器调整（请在版本上线时查看完整的更新文档），而是首先来介绍一下“全场最靓的仔”：米斯利亚2442。虽说米斯利亚是在WSTR的改动之后才开始变得主流，但事实上，这把枪一直是非常好用、非常高人气的选项，而且在它持续称霸天仓五地表之前就已经受到了我们的关注。

我们对米斯利亚的最大改动并不在这件武器本身，而是移除了顺滑弹匣模组的射速加成。这套模组可以说是我们开展的一项有趣实验，毕竟米斯利亚是唯一能安装这些模组的武器。但事实证明，该设计的可持续性不强。依照原本的设计思路，米斯利亚最主要的缺点就是射速较慢。玩家可以找准节奏，在每次射击空档进行还击，从而对其进行有效反制。顺滑弹匣弥补了米斯利亚的这一缺陷。但显而易见，这种加成让这件武器以及其尊享模组过于超模了。

所以为了解决这个问题，我们进行了以下调整：

米斯利亚2442

  • 腰射时的弹道散布范围扩大10%。
  • 瞄准时的弹道散布范围扩大5%。
  • 基础射速从58RPM提高至72RPM。
    • 在顺滑弹匣模组系列提供的射速属性加成被移除的情况下，这样的改动可以稍加弥补。
  • 重新设置了弹匣属性的基准，现在每种弹匣模组提供的子弹数都至少+1发。
  • 基础弹匣容量依然是4，且最大弹匣容量依然是10。
  • 基础伤害衰减开始的距离从11米缩短到10米。
  • 最大伤害衰减开始的距离从15米延长到16米。

全自动切换器尊享模组

此模组的玩法和配装体验较为静态，所以为了优化这方面，我们做了以下调整：

  • 全自动切换器提供的射速加成从150RPM降低至90RPM。
  • 添加了+50瞄准速度和+50瞄准精准度的效果。
  • 腰射时，米斯利亚为全自动射击，弹道散布范围提升10%，且稳定性极大幅提升。
  • 在瞄准时，米斯利亚射程极大幅提升，且弹道散布范围极大幅缩小，但无法全自动射击。

我们相信这样改动能够让玩家根据与敌人的距离采用不同射击模式，利用这个尊享模组制造出更有趣的空间与距离战法，同时深挖出更大的配装潜力——例如你可以专注于提升腰射模式或瞄准模式的威力。

聊完最靓的仔，我们再来聊聊失意者。相比大受欢迎的米斯利亚，奔袭和近战战术手枪的人气就没那么高了。

先说说奔袭，我们想要保持它上手简单的特点，因为它再强化个几次就能（咳咳）彻底碾压其他步枪了。不过我们也同意，它确实需要再提升一点威力。所以在第2赛季上线时，我们会推出以下更改：

奔袭

  • 基础武器的水平和垂直后坐力降低，加强较低稀有度时的控制力：
    • 水平后坐力降低约50%。
    • 垂直后坐力降低约12%。

相比于其他步枪，高级的卓越奔袭表现依然非常强势，所以我们把重点放在了如何让大家在经济型搭配中或赞助包中使用低稀有度奔袭不那么费劲。有请赛博艾克米赞助包。

而对于近战战术手枪，这件武器的调整思路是这样的：如果你着力配置这把枪，并成功打出爆头，就能收获成功的满足感。只可惜目前为止，它并未达到预想的效果。即便配置全满，近战战术手枪依然没有足够的吸引力在大家的保险库中占得一席之地。在第2赛季，大家将体验到以下调整：

近战战术手枪

  • 这件武器射击时后坐力衰减的速度提升15%。
  • 瞄准精准度误差系数降低约50%。
  • 重新设置了瞄准辅助和准星磁性的最小和最大衰减距离基准：
    • 瞄准辅助
      • 基础射程属性下，最小距离从11米延长到14米；最大射程属性下，最小距离从14米延长到22米。
      • 基础射程属性下，最大距离从22米延长到25米；最大射程属性下，最大距离从21米延长到29米。
    • 准星磁性
      • 基础射程属性下，最小距离从15米延长到18米；最大射程属性下，最小距离从18.6米延长到29.6米。
      • 基础射程属性下，最大距离从25米延长到26.2米；最大射程属性下，最大距离从24.8米延长到39米。
  • 重新设置了瞄准辅助椎体大小属性的基准：
    • 基础瞄准辅助属性下的瞄准辅助椎体大小扩大33%。
    • 最大瞄准辅助属性下的瞄准辅助椎体大小扩大16%。
    • 为了平衡这些更改，重新设置了腰射和瞄准射弹精准度属性的基准。
      • 换而言之，就是为了平衡瞄准辅助椎体大小的提升，射弹误差也会被调高。
  • 最大蹲伏精准度属性下的蹲伏精准度加成降低117%。
  • 最小移动精准度属性下的移动精准度加成降低22%。
  • 最大移动精准度属性下的移动精准度加成提高50%。

以上就是几项主要武器改动。在第2赛季上线时，我们还会推出更多针对每件武器的具体调整。更多内容将在6月3日发布的完整更新中推出。

模组与配装

在打响第一枪之前，战斗就已经开始。要在天仓五IV生存，精心的准备和配装工作是重中之重。第2赛季让我们有机会可以回顾许多武器模组的表现，思考如何让你的选择更有意义，怎样让模组提供更多体验，而不仅仅是属性的加减。

对于第二赛季，我们针对模组的几个关键领域投入了大量精力。首先，我们会添加两个新的模组系列，和八个新的芯片系列。我们也对每一款弹匣和每一款瞄准镜模组进行了巨细靡遗的平衡性调整。限于篇幅，我们无法在这篇博文中列出全部细节，所以请在完整的更新文档中查看针对模组的所有更改的详情。

那么，我们来看看新加入的两个模组系列吧。首先就是上面提到过的折叠枪托：

折叠枪托是一种全新的模组系列，首先会适配新加入的两款武器，KKV和D54战斗手枪。折叠枪托模组和轻机枪使用的护盾模组类似，可以提供独特的玩法变化。

在装备后，折叠枪托默认处于折叠状态，在此模式下，模组可以为武器提供腰射精准度和移动速度等属性加成。然后你可以按下第二武器动作键，展开枪托，从腰射相关加成切换到瞄准精准度和射程方面的加成。这种模组可以为同一件武器提供不同的游戏风格选项，以及在战斗中即时更改策略的能力，我们对此非常满意。

其次，我们还会添加一种全新的瞄准镜模组类型，暗视瞄准镜。随着夜间沼泽地图的亮相，我们自然想要确保大家拥有必要的手段，可以在黑暗、危机四伏的环境中精准执行每一个决策。

每种武器都会获得专属的暗视瞄准镜，而且这些模组都将拥有两个稀有度：豪华和卓越。所有暗视瞄准镜的功能都类似，都会在开镜时显示全屏晕影，就和狙击枪瞄准镜的效果一样，只不过所有武器都能用。此外，在瞄准时，你还可以切换输出，启动夜视效果，提高观察四周的亮度。卓越版本的夜视效果视野角度更大，距离也更远。我们的目的是让这种模组的独特效果不仅在夜间沼泽提供辅助，还能应用到游戏的其他部分。

武器芯片是武器配装的主要元素，这种软件升级可以让你的武器化不可能为可能。在芯片方面，我们确实有很多工作要做。随着第2赛季上线，我们计划先下线一些效果和表现不符预期的芯片系列，不只针对表现过差的，也有表现过强的：

  • 堆叠溢出
    • 所有稀有度都从第2赛季的掉落池中下线。
  • 最优主导
    • 所有稀有度都从第2赛季的掉落池中下线。

堆叠溢出在部分武器上表现过强，而在其他一些情况下又远远未达预期。所以我们决定将这个芯片暂时召回。至于最优主导，虽然从配装的角度来看，它拥有有趣的潜力，但没能在我们预期设计的生态位上站稳脚跟，所以它也会暂时回到替补席上。

不过别担心，八个新的芯片系列将会登场亮相，助你大展身手。这里就不全部剧透了，先介绍几个亮点：

  • 警报主义者（所有稀有度）
    • 短暂瞄准后，射弹命中会触发骇人的扫描无人机警报。
      • 我们知道大家非常喜欢鸟类学家，但不得不承认，在低温档案室听到鸟叫确实有点违和。也许这个芯片能改善这个问题？
  • 废料场（所有稀有度）
    • 使用该武器击败敌对目标时，有几率生成已耗尽的治疗物品或标准材料。
  • 思维冻结（仅卓越稀有度）
    • 使用该武器完成精准击倒或击杀时，引发一次小型爆炸，并对附近敌对目标施加结霜效果。

已有的芯片也会得到一些改动。同样，限于篇幅，我们无法全面介绍，只能先帮大家管中窥豹：

  • 赏金猎人
    • 提高每击杀一名UESC单位获得的信用点：
      • 标准：从10信用点提高至25信用点。
      • 强化：从15信用点提高至50信用点。
      • 豪华：从20信用点提高至75信用点。
      • 卓越：从25信用点提高至100信用点。
    • 修复了在摧毁UESC扫描无人机时，赏金猎人不会奖励信用点的错误。
  • 云裔
    • 完全重新设计了特性效果：
    • 处于烟雾中时，该武器的稳定性、精准度、操控性和移动速度被动提升。
      • 之前的效果只是在烟雾中时，填装会使弹匣溢出弹药。

最后再说一说瞄准镜和弹匣模组。游戏中的每一款瞄准镜和弹匣模组都得到了全面的属性平衡性调整。这些改动大部分是为了抹平一些表现异常的情况，同时也为了让所有使用这些瞄准镜和弹匣的武器拥有更多有趣的选择。不过有几个比较重要的大幅改动值得特别介绍一下：

  • 从第2赛季开始，游戏中将不再有仅供狙击步枪使用的热能瞄具。
    • 即便在削弱过射程后，装有热成像瞄准镜的狙击步枪威力也实在超标了。我们不会将其永久搁置，只是需要更多时间来酝酿一些对热成像玩法机制的长期更改。在有成熟的方案之前，我们觉得先将其下线比较好。
  • 从第2赛季起，每个系列的卓越弹匣模组将会默认带有新的矢量弹特性。
    • 矢量弹是一种全新的特性，其设计功能是作为一种信息工具，可以让你标记空间，追踪敌人的位移，以及在黑暗角落提供视野。
    • 短暂瞄准一段时间后，下一发射弹在击中任何物体时会释放探测脉冲，和暗视效果类似。被脉冲扫到的敌人将会在短时间内以高亮显示。

我们非常期待看到大家的配装会在第2赛季迎来什么样的变化。如需了解全部详情，请查看完整的更新文档。

装备和生存

要在疾行中确保生存，武器并不是你唯一的工具。装备也会在战斗沙盒中扮演关键角色，帮助玩家扭转不利的战局。

不过……第1赛季中的部分装备好像有一点太强了。在介绍下个赛季推出的三款新装备之前，我们想要先借此机会概述一下第2赛季开始时对装备系统进行的几项更改。

  • 手雷准备动作延长0.2秒。

准备动作指的是拔出安全销准备投掷的过程，投掷动作（松开按钮后）不变。我们相信这处更改可以保留投掷手雷的敏捷度和乐趣体验，但同时也能在相当程度上限制玩家们快速连续投掷手雷的数量。延长时间也能让我们打磨这个准备动作的动画，使其更具表现力。

  • 破片手雷
    • 最大伤害降低15%（135→115）。
  • 热量手雷
    • 伤害降低50%（40→20）。
  •  信号干扰器
    • 当玩家执行动作、使用技能、使用武器射击或受到伤害时，视觉和探测干扰会短暂关停。
    • 不再干扰预警系统（尊享侦察核心）的探测。
    • 不再干扰必要情报（风行者M1T尊享瞄准镜模组）的探测。

在第1赛季中，信号干扰器似乎成了每场战斗中的标配，因为不论你是调整站位准备伏击，还是直接冲入战斗之中，这件装备都能够确保提供直截了当的优势。我们希望这次更改能够让信号干扰器为战术性移动和潜行提供更多回报，同时在交火中的辅助作用又不那么强大。

在解决旧问题之后，我们来看看新增内容吧。

首先，我们会加入冰霜地雷。和阔刀地雷类似，冰霜地雷是一种可放置装备，当敌人接近时便会引爆。它不会像阔刀地雷那样对锥形空间内的目标造成大量伤害，而是在球形空间内爆炸，造成中等伤害，但同时也会对所有爆炸波及的目标造成结霜减益。大家一定要注意保暖啊。

其次，我们还会加入矢量手雷。这种手雷的主要功能并非造成伤害，而是收集信息。当矢量手雷爆炸时，它会释放出一片探测脉冲，并在场景中存在一小段时间，高亮显示大范围区域内的空间，以及处于其探测半径内的所有敌人。这种手雷是在夜间沼泽和其他视野受限环境中行动的必备伴侣。

最后，我们还会加入三种颜色的信号弹。信号弹包含红色、绿色、蓝色三种，每种都有不同的镜头光晕形状。这些信号弹并不强调造成伤害，而是会存在于场景之中，作为补充照明。我们的本意是让这些信号弹成为不爱出声沟通的疾行者们的交流工具。比如说在激烈的交火中打出一发绿色信号弹，也许你就能结交新的盟友呢？或者你也许可以顺着一发蓝色信号弹的指引，找到某个心善的疾行者留下的战利品呢？选择权都交到你的手中。

疾行者和植入物

你的生物合成半机械外壳是决定你游戏风格的基础。在第2赛季中，我们会迈出扩充外壳阵容的第一步，提供更多方式来探索和体验这个危机四伏、异状丛生的世界。

为大家隆重介绍哨兵。这款外壳是一名防御专家，专长在战场上划分出一小块空间，用巧妙布置的陷阱和致命的武力来提供防御保障。

哨兵的技能组主要围绕区域控制和火力承压下的应变能力而打造。它会为战场带来几件新的工具：

  • 防御系统（主要技能）：
    • 部署一座防御平台，摧毁来袭的爆炸物，保障外壳不受伤害。在设备附近时，你和盟友的武器稳定性和填装速度将获得提升。而且如往常一样，请记住它能够发射的射弹数量有限！眼观六路，方能确保无虞。
  • 陷阱地雷（战术技能）：
    • 掷出一枚感应触发地雷，敌人接近时便会引爆，释放出若干枚能够造成固化效果的子弹药。
  • 猎物追踪器（特性技能）：
    • 在你的HUD中启动运动追踪系统，让你可以在雷达上看见正前方一片锥形空间内的移动敌人。
  • 城堡原则（被动技能）：
    • 附近敌对目标数量越多，你准备并填装冲锋枪、手枪和霰弹枪的速度越快。受到溅射伤害时，你的硬件、防火墙和自我修复速度在短时间内提升。

你可以使用猎物追踪器来实时收集移动中敌人的位置信息，让你可以布置完美的伏击，或追击逃走的刺客。你可以使用陷阱地雷来阻滞冲向你的霰弹枪毁灭者，或向UESC运输舰下方投掷，立即提供群控效果。在局势变得危急时，你可以部署防御系统，拦截袭来的火箭无人机齐射，然后掏出你的KKV或米斯利亚，“热情”款待任何不速之客。

我们还会添加一整套共10种不同稀有度的哨兵专属疾行者核心。先让大家尝个鲜：

  • 逆转回春（强化）：击败处于固化状态的敌对目标后，会为你提供持续治疗。
    • 如果你缺少医疗消耗品，团队里又没有救援的话，这个核心就可以给你的配装提供一些持续作战能力。
  • 健康信标（豪华）：位于你的防御系统附近时，你和盟友使用医疗物品的速度加快。
    • 适合更强调支援的游戏风格，可以让你帮盟友在战斗中坚持更长时间。
  • 征用权（尊享）：被你的防御系统拦截的疾行者手雷会掉落在地，成为可拾取物品。
    • 对抗投掷武器滥用的终极工具，让你可以在字面意思上以其人之“雷”还治其人之身。

我们非常期待看到哨兵会对当前版本带来怎样的震撼，以及大家能打出什么样的骚气（或爆笑）操作。

植入物

接下来要介绍一下植入物。

起初我们的计划是围绕强力的疾行者专属特性设计核心，以及围绕有趣的属性增益设计植入物，借此为核心和植入物带来独特的定位。通过早期的测试，我们开始为植入物添加随机特性，而不只是属性加成，为游戏的配装玩法提供更丰富的变化。事实证明，这些设计有效地拓展了不同游戏风格的玩法深度，并且很快成为了推动围绕植入物进行选择的主要因素。

不过植入物在第1赛季的具体实装方式却带来了一系列问题。因为物品名称主要反映属性加成，而不是植入物的特性，且关于特性作用的信息又被埋在了大量的提示文本中，所以玩家无法通过观察容器中或地上的物品，直观地了解它的作用，进而导致这套系统在应用层面存在痛点。

在第2赛季中，我们会进行大幅改动，重新设计植入物的架构，以期解决这些问题。植入物的主要功能将会是提供特性，而疾行者属性加成则会成为次要元素。每一款植入物现在都会提供一个固定特性，且可以通过物品名称直接反映，同时包含一项固定的属性加成。比如说，如果你捡到一个毒素注射头部植入物，它的效果是会为你的防火墙、耐热性和坠落抗性提供加成，或者捡到一个救援斗篷躯干植入物，提供复活速度、战术技能恢复和硬件加成。

经过本次重新设计之后，你将可以获得所有和第1赛季相同的特性（如果你运气够好，也包括尊享稀有度的独特特性），外加八种第2赛季加入的全新特性，例如白衣夜行，它可以让你在使用治疗消耗品时隐形。部分植入物只存在高稀有度版本，也就是说你必须要深入难度更高的区域才能找到……或者可以从其他疾行者手里抢来。

最后要说的一点就是，这还不是植入物的最终形态。我们计划中的下一步就是通过植入物图标的设计实现一目了然的理解，而且目前我们还在酝酿更多调整，并期待在未来逐步推出，和大家见面。

最后，我们想要简单聊一聊我们对疾行者外壳进行的其他调整。

我们在本赛季的工作重心主要放在问题修复和游戏体验改善的更新上，例如对攀越机制的优化，让攀爬复杂地形的动作更稳定，让移动更顺滑，减少出现失去同步的情况，并解决死亡箱刷新到无法触及区域的问题。其中一项改动特别值得一提，那就是我们会削弱主要技能和战术技能恢复属性在100点数值时对疾行者技能冷却时间的加速效果，确保在高等级疾行者的交战中，各方都有足够的反制机会。

请关注我们的完整更新文档，了解全部改动详情，以及更多的开发者提示。

轰动完结

以上就是对第2赛季即将推出的战斗内容的简要介绍，好帮助大家在6月3日新赛季上线时能够快速投入战斗。再次提醒大家，《失落星船：马拉松》的公开试玩周将于6月3日至6月10日举行，可在Steam、PlayStation 5和Xbox Series X|S平台上体验完整游戏。

新赛季会带来更精心打磨的战斗沙盒；添加新的装备和选项；让黑暗区域更具可玩性，同时又保留其危险性；以及确保你在打开你的配装时，展现在你眼前的选择能让你感觉更需要深思熟虑、更有用、更值得探索。

我们希望这些调整能让战斗体验更直接和利落，让配装选择更具影响力，让每一次疾行都感觉能给你更多的学习和适应空间，让你找到更符合自己喜好的游戏风格。

随着这些改动即将交付到你的手中，大家的反馈也将变得至关重要。之前，大家就在通过各种方式与我们展开交流，我们会继续密切关注这些渠道，特别是我们的官方Discord、Steam讨论区、Reddit，以及我们在Twitter和Bluesky上的MarathonDevTeam社交频道。我们想要听到大家觉得哪些地方做得好，哪些地方感觉不够好，哪些地方给大家带来了惊喜，又有哪些地方体验不如预期。

狩猎愉快！
zhChs.bodyText: 今天我们想要为大家清楚详尽地介绍一下我们正在为第2赛季的战斗沙盒进行着什么样的工作。请阅读 这篇博文 ，了解更多关于第2赛季的内容，包括夜间沼泽地图、全新的疾行者外壳哨兵，以及摇篮系统。 第2赛季给了我们一个机会，让我们可以进一步打磨《失落星船：马拉松》中玩家们已经逐渐熟悉的部分，同时将整体战斗体验优化得更清晰、更直观，也让投入时间去学习能取得更大的回报。 新赛季让我们有机会全面地审视游戏中的所有部分，而非各自为政地进行单项修改。这个赛季的重点是如何让战斗呈现出整体性体验，你的武器要如何提供符合其定位的手感，你的配装会如何改变你所做的决策，新的工具在局面变得棘手时能如何帮助你生存，以及你的疾行者外壳的技能组在每一场战斗中要如何体现出更鲜明的特色。 在第2赛季中，我们的工作重心放在了武器、模组、配装、装备、生存、疾行者外壳、植入物，以及整体的沙盒上。对以上所有领域的调整，都将基于一个统一的标准：我们希望让你的选择感觉更有目的性，让我们的深层系统互动起来更容易，以及让你的配装在天仓五的战场中感觉真的能起到重要作用。 那么我们就一起来看看吧。 武器和沙盒 我们先说说最热闹的部分。 在第2赛季上线初期，我们的工作重心主要是让武器更易使用，并强化武器的设计定位。我们希望当大家在使用或对抗每一类武器时，能觉得这些武器的功能和作用更明晰，且彼此之间区别足够明显。 这项工作涉及广泛的调整，包括弹药经济、击杀时间以及射程特性，比如让近程武器在近距离上更致命，让中程武器能够帮助你更有效地防守阵地，以及让武器定位能够在每场比赛中更加易于理解。 第2赛季同时也会为游戏加入几款新武器： 一些眼尖的《失落星船：马拉松》老粉也许已经注意到了，我们的其中一件新武器看上去有点眼熟。在第2赛季加入的KKV-9SD是一把手枪架构的冲锋枪，自带内置抑制器，是与夜间沼泽的恐怖威胁作战的完美伴侣。也就是说这把枪可以搭配手枪瞄准镜、手枪弹匣模组，以及手枪芯片。它拥有游戏实装的所有武器中最快的射速，在近距离战斗中效果极好，特别是可以用来扫倒那些爱冲锋的霰弹枪突袭者。它的尊享模组，镖弹鼓匣，可以在你击破敌对目标的护盾时为你提供治疗，进而提升你的战场生存能力。 我们为第2赛季推出的第二件武器是一款全自动三连发手枪，D54战斗手枪。这把手枪的定位介于手枪和马格南两种泛型之间，在1对1决斗中拥有一定优势。它可以和其他威力较大的武器搭配使用，是非常优秀的随身佩枪。在紧要关头，不必浪费时间填装你的狙击枪或霰弹枪，切换到D54战斗手枪，一样能帮助你杀出一条血路。它的尊享模组，亡命者枪托，可以发挥出全新的折叠枪托模组的优势：在折叠状态下，击败敌人可以积攒最多两层效果，而在展开状态下，D54战斗手枪的射击模式则会转变为一次射出9发子弹的超级爆发连射，只要还有效果层数，便可持续射击。文章后段会详细介绍折叠枪托的用法。 我们对改动的预期十分简单和明确。我们希望让玩家对武器的选择更深思熟虑，也希望你能够毫无阻碍地感觉到每款武器的特殊之处，让你可以主动选择你想要体验的战斗。
